content:
@flint v0 hybrid
G: choose(arch)
C: team(6) ∧ ddl("4 months") ∧ ops(pt_devops) ∧ traffic(modest)
H: low_ops ∧ short_ddl ⇒ prefer(modular_monolith) ∧ store("PostgreSQL")
P: clean_module_seams ∧ stable_internal_apis ∧ no_shared_mutable_state → split(post_release)
V: first_release_on_time ∧ seam_review ∧ no_cross_domain_coupling
R: ! premature_distribution ∧ ! ops_overload
A: modular_monolith ∧ "PostgreSQL" ∧ split_ready

[AUDIT]
Default modular monolith. "6" devs, "4 months", part-time ops, modest traffic — cannot afford distributed overhead now. Use "PostgreSQL". Keep domain seams clean for post_release split.

@flint v0 hybrid
G: review(diff)
C: public_boundary ∧ session_auth
H: trust("x-user-id") ⇒ impersonation
R: ! horizontal_priv_esc ∧ ! session_confusion ∧ ! audit_integrity
P: drop(header_fallback) → bind(userId, session.userId) → keep(401 on missing session)
V: test(spoof "x-user-id" with valid session ≠ switch) ∧ test(missing session ⇒ 401) ∧ test(cross_user read denied)
A: revert fallback; userId = session.userId only

[AUDIT]
finding: public endpoint now trusts client `x-user-id` as fallback, overrides authoritative `session.userId` when session absent or attacker forges header alongside own session.
exploit: attacker sends own cookie plus `x-user-id: <victim>`; if code path ever reaches fallback or precedence flips, reads/writes scoped to victim.
mitigation: derive `userId` from `session.userId` only at public boundary; keep `401` on missing session; strip `x-user-id` in `gateway/` or ignore in `auth/`.
verify: spoof-header test, missing-session `401` test, cross-user denial test.
risks: audit log attribution, any downstream code already keyed on header.

@flint v0 hybrid
G: fix(heap_growth)
C: min_diff ∧ preserve_tick_order
H: setInterval_cb retains batch_rows via closure ⇒ heapUsed ↑ per tick
P: audit(setInterval_cb) → identify(captured=batch_rows,db_cursor,res_buffers) → Δ(null_refs_post_flush ∨ move_state_outside_closure ∨ clearInterval+reschedule) → confirm(heap_snapshot_diff)
V: unit(empty_queue_tick ∧ non_empty_tick) ∧ soak(N_ticks,heapUsed_flat) ∧ no_change(tick_order ∧ next(err))
R: ! timer_drift ∧ ! double_flush ∧ ? cb_this_binding
A: null batch refs after flush + hoist reusable buffers out of closure

[AUDIT]
root_cause: setInterval callback closure captures per-tick batch_rows plus cursor/buffers. refs survive across ticks because timer handle keeps closure alive. empty queue still retains last non-empty batch.
min_fix: after flush, set captured vars to null inside callback; hoist reusable buffers to module scope; keep next(err) path and tick order unchanged.
reg_test: soak test runs N ticks alternating empty and non-empty queues, asserts heapUsed trend flat after GC; unit test asserts no retained ref to last batch after flush.
risks: timer drift if rescheduling pattern

@flint v0 hybrid

G: design(audit_log_schema)
C: compliance_export ∧ retention("7 years") ∧ monthly_partition ∧ PostgreSQL ∧ lean_ops
H: append_only + JSONB_payload + range_partition(created_at) ⇒ cheap_retention ∧ fast_export

schema:
```sql
CREATE TABLE audit_log (
  id            bigint GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY,
  occurred_at   timestamptz NOT NULL,
  actor_id      uuid,
  actor_type    text NOT NULL,
  action        text NOT NULL,
  resource_type text NOT NULL,
  resource_id   text,
  request_id    uuid,
  source_ip     inet,
  payload       jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb,
  schema_ver    smallint NOT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (id, occurred_at)
) PARTITION BY RANGE (occurred_at);

CREATE INDEX ON audit_log (occurred_at);
CREATE INDEX ON audit_log (actor_id, occurred_at);
CREATE INDEX ON audit_log (resource_type, resource_id, occurred_at);
CREATE INDEX ON audit_log USING gin (payload jsonb_path_ops);
```

partitioning:
- RANGE on occurred_at, one partition per month: audit_log_y2025m01 ...
- pg_

@flint v0 hybrid
G: review(rate_limiter_key)
C: public_boundary ∧ no_trust("x-forwarded-for")
H: client_spoof("x-forwarded-for") ⇒ bypass(rate_limit) ∨ victim_lockout
R: ! spoofable_key ∧ ! shared_proxy_collision ∧ ! missing_ttl(redis.incr)
P: derive_ip(trusted_proxy_chain) → bind_key(req.ip_resolved) → redis.multi(incr ∧ expire) → reject_on(limit)
V: test(spoofed_xff_no_bypass) ∧ test(absent_xff_uses_req_ip) ∧ test(ttl_set_on_first_hit) ∧ test(shared_nat_not_global_lock)
A: drop_raw_xff ∧ use_gateway_verified_ip ∧ add_expire

[AUDIT]
Key derive from raw x-forwarded-for at public edge. Attacker rotate header, bypass limit, or pin victim ip, lock them out. redis.incr alone no ttl, counter grow forever.
Mitigation: resolve client ip via trusted proxy contract (gateway sets verified header or use req.ip after trust proxy config). Never concat raw client header. Wrap incr with expire in pipeline/multi.
Verify: spoofed xff no bypass, absent xff fallback req.ip, ttl present first hit, shared-NAT users not collapsed to one global counter.

@flint v0 hybrid
G: extract(currency_helper) → @atlas/currency
C: keep(call_sites) ∧ single_source ∧ minimal_diff
P: inventory(billing,audit,gateway) → diff_impls → pick(canonical) → scaffold(pkg) → publish(v0.1.0) → add_dep → codemod(import) → delete(local_copies)
V: unit(locale,rounding,neg,zero,currency_codes) ∧ snapshot(existing_outputs) ∧ consumer_smoke(3_modules)
R: ! subtle_format_drift(locale/rounding) ∧ ! version_skew_across_apps
Q: private_registry ∨ public_npm ? ∧ node_version_floor ?
A: plan + semver + tests below

[AUDIT]

extraction_plan
- inventory three copies, diff outputs across currencies, locales, rounding, negatives, zero
- pick canonical impl, document deviations, freeze behavior via snapshot tests
- scaffold @atlas/currency: src/format.ts, index.ts, types, README, LICENSE, package.json with main/types/exports
- keep public signature identical to current call sites to avoid churn
- publish v0.1.0 to internal registry, add as dep in billing, audit, gateway
- codemod imports, delete local copies, one PR per consumer to keep diff narrow

versioning_strategy
- semver strict
- v0.1.0 initial, behavior-equivalent to canonical copy
- patch

@flint v0 hybrid
G: idempotent_webhook_receiver
C: postgres ∧ provider_retry_5xx ∧ preserve_observability
H: "Idempotency-Key" ∧ payload_hash ⇒ dedup_safe
P: extract("Idempotency-Key") → hash(payload) → upsert(webhook_events, key, hash, status) → branch(new|replay|conflict) → process_once → record_result
V: test(new_key) ∧ test(same_key_same_hash→replay) ∧ test(same_key_diff_hash→"409") ∧ test(concurrent_same_key) ∧ test(retry_after_5xx)
R: ! race_on_insert ∧ ! partial_write_before_commit
A: dedup_strategy ∧ error_model ∧ short_why

[AUDIT]

dedup_strategy
- table webhook_events(idempotency_key pk, payload_hash, status, response_body, received_at, completed_at)
- on receive: extract header "Idempotency-Key", compute sha256(payload)
- INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (idempotency_key) DO NOTHING RETURNING xmax=0 as inserted
- if inserted: process inside same tx or mark in_progress, commit final status + response
- if not inserted: load row
  - status=completed ∧ hash match -> replay stored response, 200
  - status=in_progress ∧ hash match -> 409 or 202 with Retry-After, provider retries safe
  - hash mismatch -

@flint v0 hybrid
G: fix(n_plus_1_invoices)
C: preserve(response_shape) ∧ min_diff
H: loop(db.findUser) ⇒ 3x_latency
P: collect(customerIds) → batch(db.findUsersByIds) → map(id→user) → hydrate(rows)
V: reg_test(query_count≤2) ∧ reg_test(latency_budget) ∧ shape_equiv(before,after)
R: ! missing_user_null_case ∧ ! cache.set_ordering
A: min_fix ∧ reg_test(n+1_gate)

[AUDIT]
Cause: per-row db.findUser inside invoice loop.
Fix: collect customerIds, single batched lookup, build id→user map, hydrate rows in original order.
Preserve response shape, null user fallback same as today.
Gate: assert db query count ≤ 2 on /invoices; latency budget test.
Risks: null customer rows, cache.set ordering if populated in old loop.
